islands.name: Dotgraph Atoll
islands.description: 1960s vector dawn — white dots on black skies. Plot savings goals like connect-the-dots constellations while rival captains race for Ledger Seals.

learning_objectives: Create a basic monthly budget from a fixed income
Categorize expenses into fixed, variable, and discretionary
Understand opportunity cost when making spending choices
Practice adjusting a budget when unexpected expenses arise
provenance.originality_notes: Theme: coastal peninsula with neighborhood districts (not a spreadsheet or office). Verb: allocate budget across NPC requests and drag-sort expenses (not passive income collection). Structure: branching quest scenarios where budget choices affect NPC outcomes (not competitive or score-based). Combines budget-allocation puzzles with story-driven NPC consequence chains.

areas.name: Main Street
areas.description: The heart of the peninsula. Shops, a post office, and a community board with odd jobs.

areas.name: Budget Bureau
areas.description: A cozy office with whiteboards and pie charts. The town planner works here.

areas.name: Rainy Day Park
areas.description: A peaceful park with a fountain. Locals come here to think about unexpected expenses.

items.name: First Paycheck
items.description: Your very first earnings! Now you need to decide how to use them.

items.name: Budget Planner
items.description: A notebook to track income and expenses. The foundation of smart money management.

items.name: Rainy Day Fund
items.description: A small emergency stash. You'll be glad you have it when surprises happen!

items.name: Peninsula Budget Badge
items.description: Awarded for mastering the basics of budgeting.

quests.title: Budget Basics
quests.description: Collect your first paycheck from Pat, then visit Planner Priya to learn how to split it into categories.
quests.hint: Start on Main Street, talk to Payroll Pat, then head to the Budget Bureau.

quests.title: Expect the Unexpected
quests.description: Coach Carlos has a surprise expense challenge. Learn why an emergency fund matters.
quests.hint: Head to Rainy Day Park after completing Budget Basics.

quests.title: Inbox Storm
quests.description: Your inbox is overflowing with real-life money decisions. Open each message, weigh the options, and make smart choices before time runs out!
quests.hint: Head to Main Street and check your inbox. Think before you click!

text.explorer: Hi! You earned your first paycheck! Let's figure out how to split it up so you can buy fun stuff AND save some too.
text.apprentice: Hey there! You've been working hard and your first paycheck is ready. But before you spend it all, have you thought about where each dollar should go?
text.strategist: Congratulations on your first paycheck. Before you allocate funds, consider the 50/30/20 rule — needs, wants, and savings. How you distribute this income now sets the tone for your financial habits.

choices.text: I want to learn how to budget!

nodes.speaker: Payroll Pat
nodes.text: Smart move! Grab your paycheck here, then visit Planner Priya at the Budget Bureau. She's the best in town at helping people plan their spending.

nodes.speaker: Payroll Pat
nodes.text: Fair enough! But if you change your mind, Planner Priya is over at the Budget Bureau.

nodes.speaker: Planner Priya
nodes.text: Welcome to the Budget Bureau! The golden rule: split your money into three buckets — needs, wants, and savings. Ready to try?

nodes.speaker: Planner Priya
nodes.text: Drag each expense into the right bucket. Rent and food are needs. Entertainment is a want. And always put something into savings!

nodes.speaker: Planner Priya
nodes.text: Needs are things you must pay for — housing, food, transport. Wants are things you enjoy but could skip. Savings is money you set aside for the future. The trick is balancing all three!

nodes.speaker: Coach Carlos
nodes.text: Life throws curveballs! What if your phone breaks or you need new shoes? That's why you need a Rainy Day Fund. Want to build one?

nodes.speaker: Coach Carlos
nodes.text: An emergency fund is money you don't touch unless something unexpected happens. Even saving a little each week adds up. Go talk to Vendor Vee — she'll show you what surprise expenses look like!

nodes.speaker: Coach Carlos
nodes.text: Bold strategy! But trust me, surprises always come. Swing by when you're ready.

nodes.speaker: Vendor Vee
nodes.text: Oh no, the town fountain just broke and we need supplies to fix it! This is exactly the kind of surprise expense Coach Carlos was talking about. Good thing we planned ahead!

choices.text: Good thing I have my Rainy Day Fund!

nodes.speaker: Vendor Vee
nodes.text: See? That's the power of an emergency fund. You handled a surprise without going into debt. Impressive!

nodes.speaker: Vendor Vee
nodes.text: 30 coins for parts. If you'd been saving a bit each week, you'd have this covered no problem. That's the lesson!

minigames.name: Budget Splitter
minigames.description: Drag expense cards into Needs, Wants, and Savings buckets to balance a monthly budget.

minigames.name: Main Street Treasure Hunt
minigames.description: Walk the peninsula grid, talk to locals, and solve budget puzzles hidden in chests.

minigames.name: Inbox Storm
minigames.description: Your inbox is flooded with money decisions! Open each message, read the scenario, and choose wisely.

minigames.name: Set the Price
minigames.description: Run a lemonade stand: slide the price and watch the crowd and your profit react. Find the sweet spot.
